title: Shadow deployment versus canary rollout
description: How shadow serving (mirror, discard) and canary (small live share) differ in risk and what each validates.
body: Use when choosing how to expose a new model to production conditions. Shadow mirrors live requests to the candidate and discards its output, validating shapes and latency at zero user risk; canary routes a small real share and measures user-facing impact. Do not canary before shadow clears — a crashing candidate then hits real users. Actions: shadow first to confirm I/O and latency, then canary with guardrails to measure outcomes. Validation signals include a shadow trace matching production shapes and a canary report on the primary metric. Risk boundary: skipping shadow exposes users to integration faults; if shadow surfaces a contract break, deny the canary and escalate.
